questions.question: Do vaccines cause food allergies in children?
questions.rationale: This directly addresses a specific vaccine misconception that contributes to vaccine hesitancy, with clear scientific evidence refuting the causal link
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many parents believe that childhood vaccines cause or increase the risk of food allergies, leading some to delay or refuse vaccinations
questions.public_stakes: Vaccine hesitancy based on this false belief leaves children vulnerable to preventable diseases while providing no protection against food allergies

questions.question: Are IgG food sensitivity tests reliable for diagnosing food problems?
questions.rationale: This addresses widespread commercial misinformation about unvalidated diagnostic tests that can cause real dietary and health harm
questions.misinformation_narrative: Commercial IgG food sensitivity tests are heavily marketed as accurate diagnostic tools, leading people to unnecessarily restrict diets based on false positive results
questions.public_stakes: People waste money on unreliable tests and may develop nutritional deficiencies from unnecessary food restrictions, while missing actual food allergies or intolerances
